llama-news.headline: Philosophers Debate Whether Llamas Can Have True Knowledge of Hay
llama-news.summary: The Third International Congress of Llama Philosophy produced no consensus on the Hay Problem: whether any llama can have genuine knowledge of hay, or only of their experience of it.
llama-news.body:
PHILOSOPHY QUARTER — The Third International Congress of Llama Philosophy concluded without consensus on the Hay Problem: whether direct knowledge of hay is possible, or whether all such knowledge is mediated through experience.

Professor Beatrice of the Patagonian School opened the debate: 'You taste the hay. But do you taste the hay, or your tasting of it?'

Aaron, speaking from the floor, offered a characteristically terse response: 'I have eaten a great deal of hay. I know it rather well.' The room erupted.

More seriously, the congress produced a working paper proposing that the distinction between propositional knowledge and experiential knowledge — knowing that hay exists versus knowing what hay is like — may be foundational to a broader theory of llama cognition. The paper is available in the Llamaverse Philosophical Archive.
